top_categories.workout_context: At mile 60 of 100, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
10,000ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Gran Fondo Maryland's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before September.
100ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
Gran Fondo Maryland's brutally hilly forested mountain roads forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 100 miles.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 100 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Gran Fondo Maryland. Train it until it feels boring.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 10,000ft of climbing on brutally hilly forested mountain roads does to your power file on race day.
100 miles rewards fat oxidation. This volume work shifts your fuel mix so you're still burning fat at hour 5, not bonking at hour 3.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on brutally hilly forested mountain roads. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
At Gran Fondo Maryland, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
Two threshold sessions per week without the recovery cost of VO2max work. For 100 miles, sustainable FTP gains matter more than peak power.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for Gran Fondo Maryland’s 100 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at Gran Fondo Maryland. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, VO2max, and Race Simulation to prepare you for 100 miles of brutally hilly forested mountain roads in Frederick, Maryland, USA.
